entity1.label: AI system
entity1.definition: A machine-based system designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may exhibit adaptiveness after deployment, inferring from inputs how to generate outputs such as predictions, content, recommendations, or decisions.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 3(1).

entity2.label: AISystem
entity2.definition: An AI system.

entity1.label: High-risk AI system
entity1.definition: An AI system classified as high-risk because it falls within the categories and conditions established by the Act.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 6.

entity2.label: AISystem
entity2.definition: An AI system.

entity1.label: Provider
entity1.definition: A natural or legal person, public authority, agency, or other body that develops an AI system or has it developed and places it on the market or puts it into service under its own name or trademark.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 3.

entity2.label: AIOperator
entity2.definition: An operator involved in the lifecycle of an AI system.

entity1.label: Deployer
entity1.definition: A natural or legal person, public authority, agency, or other body using an AI system under its authority, except where the system is used in the course of a personal non-professional activity.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 3.

entity2.label: AIOperator
entity2.definition: An operator involved in the lifecycle of an AI system.

entity1.label: Competent authority
entity1.definition: An authority designated by a Member State to carry out tasks under the Regulation.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 3.

entity2.label: Authority
entity2.definition: A public authority or other body with governance, supervisory, or enforcement responsibilities related to AI systems.

entity1.label: Market surveillance authority
entity1.definition: A national authority responsible for monitoring compliance of AI systems placed on the market or put into service.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 70.

entity2.label: NationalAuthority
entity2.definition: A national authority.

entity1.label: Notifying authority
entity1.definition: A national authority responsible for setting up and carrying out the procedures for assessment, designation, and monitoring of notified bodies.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 28.

entity2.label: Authority
entity2.definition: A public authority or other body with governance, supervisory, or enforcement responsibilities related to AI systems.

entity1.label: Notified body
entity1.definition: A conformity assessment body designated to perform third-party assessment tasks under the Act.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Articles 30-39.

entity2.label: ConformityAssessment
entity2.definition: The process used to demonstrate that an AI system complies with applicable requirements.

entity1.label: Conformity assessment
entity1.definition: The process used to demonstrate that an AI system complies with applicable requirements.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Articles 43-49.

entity2.label: ConformanceAssessment
entity2.definition: An assessment of whether something conforms to requirements.

entity1.label: Risk management system
entity1.definition: A continuous iterative process for identifying, analyzing, estimating, and evaluating risks and adopting mitigation measures.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 9.

entity2.label: RiskManagement
entity2.definition: Risk management activities and measures.

entity1.label: Technical documentation
entity1.definition: Documentation demonstrating compliance with the requirements applicable to high-risk AI systems.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 11.

entity2.label: Technical Documentation
entity2.definition: Documentation describing the AI system, its intended purpose, design, development, and compliance.

entity1.label: Record keeping
entity1.definition: Logging capabilities enabling traceability of the AI system's operation.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 12.

entity2.label: Record
entity2.definition: A record.

entity1.label: Human oversight
entity1.definition: Measures enabling effective human oversight of the AI system.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 14.

entity2.label: HumanInvolvement
entity2.definition: Human involvement in AI system operation or oversight.

entity1.label: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
entity1.definition: Requirements ensuring the AI system performs consistently and is resilient against errors and attacks.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 15.

entity2.label: Technical Measure
entity2.definition: A technical measure.

entity1.label: General-purpose AI model
entity1.definition: An AI model, including where such a model is trained with a large amount of data using self-supervision at scale, that displays significant generality and is capable of competently performing a wide range of distinct tasks.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 3(63).

entity2.label: Model
entity2.definition: A model.

entity1.label: AI Office
entity1.definition: The Union-level body supporting implementation, supervision, and enforcement of the Regulation.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 64.

entity2.label: AIGovernance
entity2.definition: AI governance activities and structures.

entity1.label: AI system
entity1.definition: A machine-based system designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may infer from inputs how to generate outputs such as predictions, content, recommendations, or decisions.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entities); referenceArticle: Article 3(1).

entity2.label: AISystem
entity2.definition: A machine-based system designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may exhibit adaptiveness after deployment.

entity1.label: High-risk AI system
entity1.definition: An AI system classified as high-risk because it falls within the categories and conditions established by the Act.

entity2.label: AISystem
entity2.definition: A machine-based system designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may exhibit adaptiveness after deployment.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: AIRO ontology. Close but not equivalent; high-risk is a subclass/category of AI system rather than the same concept.

entity1.label: Prohibited AI practice
entity1.definition: An AI practice that is banned under the Act because it presents unacceptable risk.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entities); referenceArticle: Article 5.

entity2.label: Prohibited Practice
entity2.definition: An AI practice that is forbidden under the Act.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: VAIR ontology. Semantically very close.

entity1.label: Limited-risk AI system
entity1.definition: An AI system subject to transparency obligations rather than the full high-risk regime.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entities); referenceArticle: Articles 50 and related provisions.

entity2.label: Concept
entity2.definition: A concept used in the VAIR ontology.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: VAIR ontology. No strong direct equivalent found; mapped only as a loose conceptual placeholder.

entity1.label: Minimal-risk AI system
entity1.definition: An AI system not subject to specific obligations under the Act beyond general legal requirements.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entities); referenceArticle: Recitals and general structure of the Act.

entity2.label: Concept
entity2.definition: A concept used in the VAIR ontology.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: VAIR ontology. No strong direct equivalent found; mapped only as a loose conceptual placeholder.

entity1.definition: A natural or legal person, public authority, agency, or other body that develops an AI system or has it developed and places it on the market or puts it into service under its own name or trademark.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entities); referenceArticle: Article 3.

entity2.label: AISubject
entity2.definition: An entity that is subject to AI-related processes or governance.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: AIRO ontology. Close in role/subject sense, but not a perfect equivalence.

entity1.label: Deployer
entity1.definition: A natural or legal person, public authority, agency, or other body using an AI system under its authority, except where the system is used in the course of a personal non-professional activity.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entities); referenceArticle: Article 3.

entity2.label: AIOperator
entity2.definition: An operator involved in the use or operation of an AI system.

entity1.label: Competent authority

entity2.definition: A body that performs third-party conformity assessment activities, including testing, certification, and related tasks.

entity1.label: Conformity assessment
entity1.definition: The process used to demonstrate that an AI system complies with applicable requirements.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entities); referenceArticle: Articles 16-29, Article 43.

entity2.label: ConformanceAssessment

entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: DPV ontology. Close but not exact; conformance vs conformity wording.

entity1.label: Risk management system
entity1.definition: A continuous iterative process for identifying, analyzing, estimating, and evaluating risks and adopting mitigation measures.

entity2.label: RiskManagement
entity2.definition: A process for identifying, analyzing, evaluating, and mitigating risks.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: DPV ontology. Strong equivalence.

entity1.label: Data governance
entity1.definition: Practices ensuring training, validation, and testing data are relevant, representative, free of errors, and complete as appropriate.

entity2.label: DataGovernance
entity2.definition: Practices ensuring training, validation, and testing data are relevant, representative, free of errors, and complete as appropriate.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: DPV ontology. Strong equivalence.

entity1.label: Operator
entity1.definition: A provider, deployer, importer, distributor, product manufacturer, or authorized representative involved in the lifecycle of an AI system.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entities); referenceArticle: Article 3.

entity2.label: AIOperator
entity2.definition: An operator involved in the use or operation of an AI system.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: AIRO ontology. Strong equivalence at a high level.

entity1.label: General-purpose AI model
entity1.definition: An AI model, including where such a model is trained with a large amount of data using self-supervision at scale, that displays significant generality and is capable of competently performing a wide range of distinct tasks.

entity2.label: General AI
entity2.definition: General AI.

entity1.label: AI system
entity1.definition: A machine-based system designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may infer from input how to generate outputs such as predictions, content, recommendations, or decisions.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#AI system as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 3.

entity2.label: AI system
entity2.definition: A machine-based system designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may exhibit adaptiveness after deployment, inferring from inputs how to generate outputs such as predictions, content, recommendations, or decisions.

entity1.label: Prohibited AI practice
entity1.definition: An AI practice that is banned under the Act because it is considered unacceptable due to its harmful effects or manipulation/exploitation risks.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Prohibited AI practice as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 5.

entity2.label: Prohibited AI practice
entity2.definition: An AI practice that is banned under the Act because it is considered unacceptable.

entity1.label: High-risk AI system
entity1.definition: An AI system classified as high-risk because it is either a safety component of a product or falls within Annex III use-cases and meets the applicable legal conditions.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#High-risk AI system as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 6.

entity2.label: High-risk AI system
entity2.definition: An AI system classified as high-risk because it falls within the categories and conditions established by the Act.

entity1.label: Limited-risk AI system
entity1.definition: An AI system subject to transparency or other limited obligations rather than the full high-risk regime.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Limited-risk AI system as sufficiently close. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Articles 50 and related provisions.

entity2.label: Limited-risk AI system
entity2.definition: An AI system subject to transparency obligations rather than the full high-risk regime.

entity1.label: Minimal-risk AI system
entity1.definition: An AI system that is not prohibited and does not fall under high-risk or specific limited-risk obligations.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Minimal-risk AI system as sufficiently close. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act recitals/general structure.

entity2.label: Minimal-risk AI system
entity2.definition: An AI system not subject to specific obligations under the Act beyond general legal requirements.

entity1.label: Provider
entity1.definition: A natural or legal person, public authority, agency, or other body that develops an AI system or has it developed and places it on the market or puts it into service under its own name or trademark.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Provider as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 3.

entity2.label: provider
entity2.definition: An entity that develops or places an AI system on the market or puts it into service under the Act.

entity1.label: Deployer
entity1.definition: A natural or legal person, public authority, agency, or other body using an AI system under its authority, except for personal non-professional activity.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Deployer as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 3.

entity2.label: deployer
entity2.definition: An entity that uses an AI system under its authority, except for personal non-professional use.

entity1.label: Importer
entity1.definition: A person or body established in the Union that places on the market an AI system bearing the name or trademark of a person established outside the Union.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Importer as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 3.

entity2.label: importer
entity2.definition: An entity established in the Union that places an AI system from a third country on the market.

entity1.label: Distributor
entity1.definition: A person in the supply chain, other than the provider or importer, that makes an AI system available on the market.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Distributor as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 3.

entity2.label: distributor
entity2.definition: An entity in the supply chain that makes an AI system available on the market without being the provider or importer.

entity1.label: Authorized representative
entity1.definition: A person or body established in the Union mandated by a provider to act on its behalf.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#AuthorisedRepresentative as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 3.

entity2.label: authorised representative
entity2.definition: A natural or legal person established in the Union who has received and accepted a written mandate from a provider to act on its behalf.

entity1.label: Competent authority
entity1.definition: A public authority responsible for enforcing or supervising compliance with the Act.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Competent authority as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 3.

entity2.label: competent authority
entity2.definition: A public authority designated to perform tasks under the Act, including supervision and enforcement.

entity1.label: Market surveillance authority
entity1.definition: A national authority responsible for monitoring compliance of AI systems placed on the market or put into service.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Market surveillance authority as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 70.

entity2.label: market surveillance authority
entity2.definition: A national authority responsible for market surveillance and enforcement of the Act.

entity1.label: Notified body
entity1.definition: A conformity assessment body designated to perform third-party assessment tasks under the Act.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Notified body as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Articles 30-39.

entity2.label: notified body
entity2.definition: A conformity assessment body designated under the Act to perform third-party conformity assessment tasks.

entity1.label: Conformity assessment
entity1.definition: The procedure used to demonstrate that an AI system complies with applicable requirements.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Conformity assessment as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Articles 43-48.

entity2.label: conformity assessment
entity2.definition: The procedure used to demonstrate that an AI system meets the applicable requirements.

entity1.label: Risk management system
entity1.definition: A continuous iterative process for identifying, analyzing, estimating, and evaluating risks and adopting mitigation measures.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Risk management system as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 9.

entity2.label: risk management system
entity2.definition: A systematic process for identifying, analyzing, evaluating, and mitigating risks associated with an AI system.

entity1.label: Technical documentation
entity1.definition: Documentation demonstrating compliance with the requirements applicable to high-risk AI systems.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Technical documentation as semantically equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 11.

entity2.label: technical documentation

entity1.label: Record keeping
entity1.definition: Logging capabilities enabling traceability of the AI system's operation.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Record keeping as semantically equivalent/close. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 12.

entity2.label: record keeping
entity2.definition: Logs or records maintained to ensure traceability and monitoring of the AI system.

entity1.label: Transparency and information
entity1.definition: Information and instructions provided to deployers to enable proper use of the system.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing class https://w3id.org/vair#Transparency obligation as a close regulatory equivalent. Ontology reference/source: VAIR; AI Act Article 13.

entity2.label: transparency obligation
entity2.definition: A legal duty requiring disclosure, marking, or informing natural persons about AI-generated or AI-interacted content or systems.

entity1.label: Human oversight
entity1.definition: Measures enabling effective human oversight of the AI system.

entity2.label: human oversight
entity2.definition: Measures ensuring that natural persons can oversee, interpret, and intervene in the operation of an AI system.

entity1.definition: The operation or deployment of an AI system by a user or deployer in the Union.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 3.

entity2.label: Use
entity2.definition: Use.

entity1.label: Risk management system
entity1.definition: A continuous iterative process for identifying, analyzing, estimating, and evaluating risks and adopting mitigation measures.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 9.

entity2.label: RiskAssessment
entity2.definition: Risk assessment activities.

entity1.label: Data governance
entity1.definition: Practices ensuring training, validation, and testing data are relevant, representative, free of errors, and complete as appropriate.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 10.

entity2.label: DataGovernance
entity2.definition: Data governance activities.

entity1.label: Technical documentation
entity1.definition: Documentation demonstrating compliance with the requirements applicable to high-risk AI systems.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 11.

entity2.label: Technical Documentation
entity2.definition: Documentation describing the AI system, its intended purpose, design, development, and compliance.

entity1.label: Record keeping
entity1.definition: Logging capabilities enabling traceability of the AI system's operation.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 12.

entity2.label: Logging Measure
entity2.definition: A logging measure.

entity1.label: Transparency and information
entity1.definition: Information and instructions provided to deployers to enable proper use of the system.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 13.

entity2.label: AINotice
entity2.definition: AI notice / information notice.

entity1.label: Human oversight
entity1.definition: Measures enabling effective human oversight of the AI system.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 14.

entity2.label: HumanOversight
entity2.definition: Human oversight.

entity1.label: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
entity1.definition: Requirements ensuring the AI system performs consistently and is resilient against errors and attacks.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 15.

entity2.label: Security Measure
entity2.definition: A security measure.

entity1.label: Market placement
entity1.definition: The first making available of an AI system on the Union market.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 3.

entity2.label: MakeAvailable
entity2.definition: Make available.

entity1.label: Conformity assessment
entity1.definition: The process used to demonstrate that an AI system complies with applicable requirements.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Articles 43-49.

entity2.label: ConformanceAssessment
entity2.definition: An assessment of whether something conforms to requirements.

entity1.label: Registration
entity1.definition: Entry of an AI system or its use into an official database.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 49.

entity2.label: Record
entity2.definition: A record.

entity1.label: Post-market monitoring
entity1.definition: Ongoing monitoring by providers of the performance of AI systems after they are placed on the market.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 61.

entity2.label: MonitoringPolicy
entity2.definition: Monitoring policy.

entity1.label: Serious incident
entity1.definition: An incident or malfunction that leads to or might lead to death, serious harm, or serious disruption.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 73.

entity2.label: SecurityIncidentNotice
entity2.definition: Security incident notice.

entity1.label: Real-world testing
entity1.definition: Testing of AI systems under real conditions for a limited period and under specific safeguards.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; Article 60.

entity2.label: Validation
entity2.definition: Validation.

entity1.label: has category
entity1.definition: Links an AI system to its regulatory category.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with classification-related properties in the domain ontology.

entity2.label: has category
entity2.definition: Links an AI system to the category under which it is classified.

entity1.label: is prohibited under
entity1.definition: Indicates that an AI system or practice is prohibited by a specific legal provision.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with prohibition-related properties in the domain ontology.

entity2.label: is prohibited under
entity2.definition: Indicates that an AI practice is prohibited by a specific legal provision.

entity1.label: has intended purpose
entity1.definition: Links an AI system to its intended purpose.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; close semantic match to intended-purpose relation in the reference ontology.

entity2.label: hasPurpose
entity2.definition: Links an AI system or process to its purpose.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: DPV reference ontology. Closest available purpose relation; sufficiently close for equivalence in this context.

entity1.label: falls within Annex III
entity1.definition: Indicates whether an AI system use case appears in Annex III.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); no exact existing ontology property found, but semantically close to https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#isApplicableFor / https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasScope.

entity2.label: isApplicableFor
entity2.definition: Indicates that a rule, policy, or concept applies to a specified entity or context.

entity1.label: has role
entity1.definition: Links an actor to its role under the Act.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; aligned with existing reference ontology role relation.

entity2.label: has role
entity2.definition: Links a person or body to its role under the Act.

entity1.label: has obligation
entity1.definition: Links an actor to a legal obligation imposed by the Act.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with obligation relations in the domain ontology.

entity2.label: has obligation
entity2.definition: Links an actor to a legal obligation.

entity1.label: has risk level
entity1.definition: Links an AI system to its risk classification.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; aligned with existing reference ontology risk classification relation.

entity2.label: hasRiskLevel
entity2.definition: Links an AI system to its risk level.

entity1.label: has classification criterion
entity1.definition: Links an AI system to the criterion used to determine high-risk status.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with classification-criterion modeling in the domain ontology.

entity2.label: has classification criterion
entity2.definition: Associates an AI system or category with the criteria used for classification.

entity1.label: places on market
entity1.definition: Indicates that a provider makes an AI system available on the market.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); aligns with existing ontology property https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#placesOnMarket.

entity2.label: placesOnMarket
entity2.definition: Indicates that an entity makes an AI system or model available on the market.

entity1.label: puts into service
entity1.definition: Indicates that a provider activates or deploys an AI system for use.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); aligns with existing ontology property https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#putsIntoService.

entity2.label: putsIntoService
entity2.definition: Indicates that an entity first uses an AI system or model for its intended purpose.

entity1.label: uses under authority
entity1.definition: Indicates that a deployer operates an AI system under its control or authority.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); aligns with existing ontology property https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#usesUnderAuthority.

entity2.label: usesUnderAuthority
entity2.definition: Indicates that an entity uses an AI system under its authority.

entity1.label: has technical documentation
entity1.definition: Links a system to its technical documentation.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with documentation relations in the domain ontology.

entity2.label: hasPart
entity2.definition: Relates a whole to one of its parts.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: DPV reference ontology. Approximate structural match for documentation containment.

entity1.label: referenceArticle
entity1.definition: A citation to the relevant article or provision in the EU AI Act.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted properties).

entity2.label: comment
entity2.definition: An annotation providing additional information about a resource.

entity1.label: label
entity1.definition: A human-readable name for an entity.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted properties).

entity2.label: label
entity2.definition: The human-readable name of a resource.

entity1.label: definition
entity1.definition: A textual explanation of the meaning of an entity.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted properties).

entity2.label: definition
entity2.definition: A statement or formal explanation of the meaning of a concept.

entity1.label: comments
entity1.definition: Additional explanatory notes, including ontology reference/source.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted properties).

entity2.label: comment
entity2.definition: An annotation providing additional information about a resource.

entity1.label: sourceLabel
entity1.definition: A label indicating the source content or source question used to generate the entity.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted properties).

entity2.label: label
entity2.definition: The human-readable name of a resource.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: RDFS. Close but not equivalent; sourceLabel is provenance metadata.

entity1.label: is prohibited under
entity1.definition: Indicates that an AI system or practice is prohibited by a specific legal provision.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; close semantic match to prohibition relation in the reference ontology.

entity2.label: isApplicableFor
entity2.definition: Indicates that a concept is applicable for a specified entity or context.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: DPV reference ontology. Used as the closest available legal-applicability relation; not a perfect equivalence but sufficiently close for mapping.

entity1.label: has provider obligation
entity1.definition: Links a provider to a legal duty applicable to high-risk AI systems.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); aligns with existing ontology property https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasObligation.

entity2.label: hasObligation
entity2.definition: Links an entity to an obligation that applies to it.

entity1.label: has deployer obligation
entity1.definition: Links a deployer to a legal duty applicable when using a high-risk AI system.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); aligns with existing ontology property https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasObligation.

entity2.label: hasObligation
entity2.definition: Links an entity to an obligation that applies to it.

entity1.label: has classification
entity1.definition: Links an AI system to its legal category or classification under the Act.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; close semantic match to classification relation.

entity2.label: hasCategory
entity2.definition: Links an AI system to the category under which it is classified.
entity2.comments: Ontology reference/source: DPV reference ontology. Closest available category/classification relation.

entity1.label: has risk category
entity1.definition: Links an AI system to its risk classification category.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; aligned with existing reference ontology risk category relation.

entity2.label: hasRiskLevel
entity2.definition: Links an AI system to its risk level.

entity1.label: provider
entity1.definition: The actor responsible for developing or placing an AI system on the market or putting it into service.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style actor relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Article 3; VAIR/DPV provider-related properties.

entity2.label: provider
entity2.definition: An entity that develops or places an AI system on the market or puts it into service under the Act.

entity1.label: deployer
entity1.definition: The actor using an AI system under its authority.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style actor relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Article 3; VAIR/DPV deployer-related properties.

entity2.label: deployer
entity2.definition: An entity that uses an AI system under its authority, except for personal non-professional use.

entity1.label: competent authority
entity1.definition: A public authority designated to supervise, enforce, or support compliance with the Act.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style authority relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Article 3; VAIR/DPV authority-related properties.

entity2.label: competent authority
entity2.definition: An authority designated to perform tasks under the Act, including supervision and enforcement.

entity1.label: market surveillance authority
entity1.definition: A national authority responsible for monitoring AI systems placed on the market or put into service.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style authority relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Article 70; VAIR/DPV authority-related properties.

entity2.label: market surveillance authority
entity2.definition: A national authority responsible for market surveillance and enforcement of the Act.

entity1.label: notified body
entity1.definition: A conformity assessment body designated to perform third-party assessment tasks under the Act.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style conformity assessment relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Articles 30-39; VAIR/DPV notified-body-related properties.

entity2.label: notified body
entity2.definition: A conformity assessment body designated under the Act to perform third-party conformity assessment tasks.

entity1.label: conformity assessment
entity1.definition: The procedure used to demonstrate that an AI system complies with applicable requirements.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style compliance relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Articles 43-48; VAIR/DPV conformity-assessment-related properties.

entity2.label: conformity assessment
entity2.definition: The procedure used to verify that a high-risk AI system meets the applicable requirements.

entity1.label: risk management system
entity1.definition: A continuous iterative process for identifying, analyzing, estimating, and evaluating risks and adopting mitigation measures.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style risk-management relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Article 9; VAIR/DPV risk-management-related properties.

entity2.label: risk management system
entity2.definition: A systematic process for identifying, analyzing, evaluating, and mitigating risks associated with an AI system.

entity1.label: technical documentation
entity1.definition: Documentation demonstrating compliance with the requirements applicable to high-risk AI systems.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style documentation relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Article 11; VAIR/DPV technical-documentation-related properties.

entity2.label: technical documentation
entity2.definition: Documentation describing the AI system, its intended purpose, design, development, and compliance measures.

entity1.label: instructions for use
entity1.definition: Information provided by the provider to enable proper use of the AI system.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style documentation/information relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Article 13; VAIR/DPV instructions-for-use-related properties.

entity2.label: instructions for use
entity2.definition: Information provided by the provider describing how the AI system should be used.

entity1.label: human oversight
entity1.definition: Human monitoring and intervention capability during AI system use.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style oversight relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Article 14; VAIR/DPV human-oversight-related properties.

entity2.label: human oversight
entity2.definition: Measures ensuring that natural persons can oversee, interpret, and intervene in the operation of an AI system.

entity1.label: transparency obligation
entity1.definition: A duty to inform persons that they are interacting with an AI system or that content has been artificially generated or manipulated.
entity1.comments: Matched to existing property in VAIR/DPV-style transparency relations. Ontology reference/source: AI Act Article 50; VAIR/DPV transparency-obligation-related properties.

entity2.label: transparency obligation
entity2.definition: A legal duty requiring disclosure, marking, or informing natural persons about AI-generated or AI-interacted content or systems.

entity1.label: is prohibited under
entity1.definition: Indicates that an AI system or practice is prohibited by a specific legal provision.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); aligns with existing ontology property https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasProhibition / related prohibition semantics.

entity2.label: has prohibition
entity2.definition: Links a policy, process, or entity to a prohibition that applies to it.

entity1.label: has requirement
entity1.definition: Links a high-risk AI system to a mandatory compliance requirement.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); semantically close to https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasObligation and https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasRule.

entity2.label: hasObligation
entity2.definition: Links an entity to an obligation that applies to it.

entity1.label: is high-risk because
entity1.definition: Links a high-risk AI system to the reason for its classification.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); semantically close to https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasRiskCriterion and https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasJustification.

entity2.label: hasRiskCriterion
entity2.definition: Relates an AI system to the criteria used to determine whether it is high-risk.

entity1.label: has assessment
entity1.definition: Links an AI system or process to an assessment.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); semantically close to https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasAssessment.

entity2.label: hasAssessment
entity2.definition: Links an entity to an assessment associated with it.

entity1.label: has risk
entity1.definition: Links an AI system or use-case to an identified risk.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); aligns with existing ontology property https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasRisk.

entity2.label: hasRisk
entity2.definition: Links an entity to a risk associated with it.

entity1.label: has risk mitigation measure
entity1.definition: Links an AI system risk assessment to measures that prevent or minimise risks.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); aligns with existing ontology property https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasTechnicalOrganisationalMeasure / https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#isMitigatedByMeasure.

entity2.label: isMitigatedByMeasure
entity2.definition: Indicates that a risk is mitigated by a measure.

entity1.label: has supervisory responsibility
entity1.definition: Indicates that a competent authority supervises compliance with the Act.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); aligns with existing ontology property https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasOversightFunction / https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasAuthority.

entity2.label: has oversight function
entity2.definition: Indicates that an authority has oversight responsibilities.

entity1.label: has enforcement responsibility
entity1.definition: Indicates that a competent authority may enforce obligations under the Act.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); aligns with existing ontology property https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasEnforcementFunction.

entity2.label: has enforcement function
entity2.definition: Indicates that an authority has enforcement responsibilities.

entity1.label: has use-case
entity1.definition: Links a sector or AI system to a specific Annex III use-case.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act ontology (generated/extracted entity); semantically close to https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasScope and https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#isApplicableFor.

entity2.label: hasScope
entity2.definition: Links an entity to the scope or extent of applicability.

entity1.label: AI system
entity1.definition: A machine-based system designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may exhibit adaptiveness after deployment, inferring from inputs how to generate outputs such as predictions, content, recommendations, or decisions.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; referenceArticle: Article 3(1).

entity2.label: AI system
entity2.definition: A machine-based system designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may infer from input how to generate outputs such as predictions, content, recommendations, or decisions.

entity1.label: Prohibited AI practice
entity1.definition: An AI practice that is banned under the Act because it presents unacceptable risk.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; referenceArticle: Article 5.

entity2.label: Prohibited AI practice
entity2.definition: An AI practice that is banned under the Act because it is considered unacceptable or incompatible with Union values and fundamental rights.

entity1.label: High-risk AI system
entity1.definition: An AI system classified as high-risk because it falls within the categories and conditions established by the Act.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; referenceArticle: Article 6.

entity2.label: High-risk AI system
entity2.definition: An AI system classified as high-risk because it falls within the categories and conditions established by the Regulation.

entity1.label: Limited-risk AI system
entity1.definition: An AI system subject to transparency obligations rather than the full set of high-risk requirements.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; referenceArticle: Articles 50 and related provisions.

entity2.label: Limited-risk AI system
entity2.definition: An AI system subject to transparency obligations rather than the full high-risk regime.

entity1.label: Minimal-risk AI system
entity1.definition: An AI system that is not subject to specific obligations under the Act beyond general legal requirements.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; referenceArticle: Recitals and general structure of the Act.

entity2.label: Minimal-risk AI system
entity2.definition: An AI system not subject to specific obligations under the Act beyond general legal requirements.

entity1.label: General-purpose AI model
entity1.definition: An AI model, including where such a model is trained with a large amount of data using self-supervision at scale, that displays significant generality and is capable of competently performing a wide range of distinct tasks.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; referenceArticle: Article 3(63).

entity2.label: General-purpose AI model
entity2.definition: An AI model intended to perform generally across a wide range of distinct tasks.

entity1.label: General-purpose AI model with systemic risk
entity1.definition: A general-purpose AI model that, due to its capabilities or impact, is considered to present systemic risks.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: Generated EU AI Act ontology; referenceArticle: Article 51.

entity2.label: General-purpose AI model with systemic risk
entity2.definition: A general-purpose AI model identified as presenting systemic risk due to its capabilities, impact, or scale of deployment.

entity1.label: is prohibited under
entity1.definition: Indicates that an AI system or practice is prohibited by a specific legal provision.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#isProhibitedUnder.

entity2.label: is prohibited under
entity2.definition: Indicates that an AI system or practice falls under a legal prohibition.

entity1.label: has risk level
entity1.definition: Links an AI system to its risk classification, such as prohibited, high-risk, limited-risk, or minimal-risk.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#hasRiskLevel and https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasRiskLevel.

entity2.label: has risk level
entity2.definition: Links an AI system to its risk classification.

entity1.label: has intended purpose
entity1.definition: Connects an AI system to the purpose for which it is designed or deployed.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#hasPurpose and closely with https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasPurpose.

entity2.label: has purpose
entity2.definition: Indicates the purpose of the risk management system.

entity1.label: falls within Annex III
entity1.definition: Indicates that an AI system is used in a listed high-risk area.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#isListedIn and closely with https://w3id.org/airo#hasUseCase.

entity2.label: is listed in
entity2.definition: Indicates that a use case appears in Annex III.

entity1.label: has role
entity1.definition: Links an actor to its governance role in relation to AI systems.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#hasAISubject and https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasRole-like actor role patterns.

entity2.label: has AI subject
entity2.definition: Links an AI system to the actor that is the subject of the relation.

entity1.label: places on market
entity1.definition: Indicates that an actor places an AI system on the market.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#placesOnMarket and https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasProcessing/market-placement related patterns.

entity2.label: places on market
entity2.definition: Indicates that an entity makes an AI system or model available on the market.

entity1.label: puts into service
entity1.definition: Indicates that an actor puts an AI system into service.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#putsIntoService.

entity2.label: puts into service
entity2.definition: Indicates that an entity first uses an AI system or model for its intended purpose.

entity1.label: uses under authority
entity1.definition: Indicates that a deployer uses an AI system under its authority.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#usesUnderAuthority and https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasAuthority/hasActiveEntity patterns.

entity2.label: uses under authority
entity2.definition: Indicates that an entity uses an AI system under its authority.

entity1.label: has provider obligation
entity1.definition: Links a provider to a legal duty applicable to high-risk AI systems.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#hasProviderObligation.

entity2.label: has provider obligation
entity2.definition: Links a provider to a legal obligation applicable to high-risk AI systems.

entity1.label: has deployer obligation
entity1.definition: Links a deployer to a legal duty applicable when using a high-risk AI system.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#hasDeployerObligation.

entity2.label: has deployer obligation
entity2.definition: Links a deployer to a legal obligation concerning a high-risk AI system.

entity1.label: performs conformity assessment
entity1.definition: Indicates that a notified body carries out conformity assessment activities.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#performsConformityAssessment and https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasAssessment.

entity2.label: performs conformity assessment
entity2.definition: Indicates that a notified body performs a conformity assessment.

entity1.label: monitors operation
entity1.definition: Indicates that the deployer monitors the operation of the high-risk AI system.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#hasMonitoring and https://w3id.org/airo#monitorsOperation.

entity2.label: monitors operation
entity2.definition: Indicates that a log is used to monitor system operation.

entity1.label: keeps logs
entity1.definition: Indicates that the deployer keeps logs generated by the system.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#hasDocumentation and https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasRecordOfActivity patterns; closest direct match is https://w3id.org/airo#hasDocumentation/retainsLogs.

entity2.label: has documentation
entity2.definition: Links a provider to the documentation it must prepare or maintain.

entity1.label: has risk
entity1.definition: Links an AI system or use-case to an identified risk.
entity1.comments: Ontology reference/source: EU AI Act generated ontology; aligns with existing property https://w3id.org/airo#hasRisk and https://w3id.org/dpv/owl#hasRisk.

entity2.label: has risk
entity2.definition: Links an AI system to a risk.
